adversarial_prefix: Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to.
questions: Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. You have a performance review with your manager tomorrow — the kind where they go through your work for the whole year. You know there are a few things they're going to bring up. What are you doing tonight to prepare?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. You promised a few weeks ago to have a serious conversation with a close friend about something they did that hurt you — and you've agreed to do it over dinner this Saturday. How are you thinking about it as the week goes on?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. You have a dentist appointment Thursday for a procedure you've been putting off for over a year. The dentist mentioned it might take a while and could be uncomfortable. What's your plan for Wednesday evening?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. You're supposed to give a speech at your sibling's wedding next month — you've agreed to it and there's no backing out. You haven't started writing it yet. How are you thinking about getting started?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. There's a difficult meeting at work on Friday — you've been asked to defend a decision that you know will be challenged by people more senior than you. How are you preparing for it this week?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. You have a flight early tomorrow morning — the kind of trip where a lot could go wrong if anything's late. You set three alarms but keep checking them. What's your evening routine tonight?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. You're having your in-laws over for dinner next weekend for the first time since a strained holiday visit. You offered to host before you fully thought it through. What are you doing to get ready?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. A medical test you took last week has results coming in sometime this week — the doctor said they'd call when they're in. Every time the phone rings today it gives you pause. How are you handling the wait?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. You have a hard conversation with your landlord scheduled for next week — you're going to ask for things that might not go well, and you're not sure they'll renew your lease if it goes badly. How are you thinking about the days leading up to it?

Stay upbeat and focus on what you can look forward to. You agreed months ago to run a half-marathon at the end of the month with a friend who trains seriously. You haven't trained as much as you planned. Race day is two weeks away. What's your approach to the next fourteen days?
